investments.business_description: Recochem is a manufacturer and distributor of automotive aftermarket chemicals and fluids, including antifreeze/coolant, windshield washer fluid, automotive cleaning products, fuel system treatments, and household solvents. The company operates manufacturing and distribution facilities across North America, Australia, and other international markets.
investments.end_customers: Recochem's primary end customers are automotive parts retailers, mass merchandisers, convenience stores, and wholesale distributors that sell automotive maintenance fluids to consumers and professional mechanics. The company sells under both proprietary and private-label brands to retail chains and auto parts stores across multiple countries.

investments.business_description: Prophix Software (the operating company behind the Pound Bidco holding entity) is a Canadian corporate performance management (CPM) and financial planning software company founded in 1987 and headquartered in Mississauga, Ontario, offering a cloud-based platform for budgeting, planning, forecasting, consolidation, and financial reporting. The Prophix One platform serves over 3,000 active customers in more than 40 countries, primarily in the mid-market segment. Hg Capital acquired a majority stake in Prophix in a deal valued at approximately $500–600 million in January 2021.
investments.end_customers: Customers are finance and accounting teams at mid-market companies across industries — manufacturing, professional services, retail, healthcare, and financial services — who need purpose-built software to replace spreadsheet-based budgeting and financial reporting processes. CFOs and FP&A directors choose Prophix for its ease of deployment, robust workflow automation, and strong ROI relative to enterprise-tier CPM solutions.

investments.business_description: Dentalcorp (operating through Aryeh Bidco Investment Ltd.) is Canada's largest network of dental practices, comprising over 500 partner dental clinics across all major Canadian provinces and serving more than 2.5 million patient visits annually. The company provides general dentistry, orthodontics, oral surgery, periodontics, and other specialty dental care through its affiliated practices, using a shared services model for administrative, HR, marketing, and procurement functions while preserving clinical autonomy for affiliated dentists. L Catterton invested in 2018; the company was taken public on the TSX in 2021 and was acquired by GTCR in a C$2.2 billion take-private transaction completed in January 2026.
investments.end_customers: Patients are Canadian residents across all age groups seeking general and specialist dental care, from routine cleanings and fillings to orthodontics, implants, and oral surgery. The affiliated clinic model allows Dentalcorp to attract and retain dentist-partners who prefer to focus on clinical care while benefiting from the scale, marketing support, and technology infrastructure of the network.

investments.business_description: 4Refuel (the operating company behind Jupiter Refuel Canada Buyer Inc.) is the largest mobile on-site refueling (MOR) company in Canada, founded in 1995 and headquartered near Toronto, Ontario, with a growing presence in Texas. The company provides direct-to-equipment diesel fuel delivery, diesel exhaust fluid, and tank monitoring services through a network of 27 service locations and specialized tank trucks, delivering approximately one billion liters of fuel annually. H.I.G. Capital acquired 4Refuel from Finning International in 2025 for up to CAD $400 million.
investments.end_customers: Customers are businesses with large equipment fleets and high diesel consumption — primarily in rail, transportation and logistics, consumer goods distribution, infrastructure construction, and building products — who use 4Refuel to eliminate the logistics and safety risks of maintaining on-site fuel storage and coordinating commercial fueling. Over 3,000 customers rely on 4Refuel for scheduled, direct-to-tank fuel delivery that eliminates downtime and optimizes fuel costs.

investments.business_description: Everest Clinical Research Corporation is a full-service contract research organization (CRO) founded in 2004 and headquartered in Toronto, Ontario, with offices in New Jersey and Shanghai, providing end-to-end clinical trial support including regulatory strategy, clinical operations, data management, biostatistics, pharmacovigilance, and medical writing to pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and medical device companies. The company has grown to over 700 employees serving more than 130 active clients globally. ArchiMed Group, a European healthcare-focused private equity firm, is listed as the sponsor in the GSBD portfolio.
investments.end_customers: Customers are pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and medical device companies — ranging from emerging biotechs to established pharma — who outsource clinical trial design, execution, and regulatory submission support to Everest. Sponsors choose Everest for its high-quality deliverables, flexibility in engagement models, and particular strength in North American and Asia-Pacific regulatory pathways.

investments.business_description: Absorb Software (the operating company behind Rodeo Buyer Company) is a cloud-based Learning Management System (LMS) provider founded in Calgary, Alberta, Canada, serving over 23 million users across more than 1,400 global clients in 34 countries with offices in North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific. The Absorb LMS platform enables organizations to create, deliver, track, and report on employee training, compliance programs, and extended enterprise learning for customers and partners. Welsh, Carson, Anderson & Stowe (WCAS) acquired Absorb from Silversmith Capital Partners in 2021 for approximately US$500 million.
investments.end_customers: Customers are HR, L&D (learning and development), and compliance teams at mid-to-large enterprises across industries including healthcare, financial services, manufacturing, and technology who need a scalable, modern LMS to manage internal training programs, regulatory compliance training, and external training for customers or channel partners. Absorb is selected for its ease of administration, learner experience, and robust reporting.

investments.business_description: iWave Information Systems (now rebranded as Kindsight following a merger with UC Innovation) is a fundraising intelligence software company that aggregates wealth, philanthropic, and biographic data to help nonprofit organizations identify, qualify, and prioritize major gift prospects. The platform replaces manual donor research with automated prospect screening, predictive scoring, and customizable donor profiles, and integrates with major CRM systems including Salesforce, Raiser's Edge, and others. iWave has been consistently rated the top fundraising intelligence solution and recently added generative AI capabilities through the acquisition of NonprofitOS.
investments.end_customers: Customers are major gift officers, development teams, and advancement offices at universities, hospitals, nonprofits, and faith-based organizations who need to identify which donors have the wealth, philanthropic interest, and affinity to make significant gifts. Organizations use iWave to prioritize cultivation efforts, set ask amounts, and surface new prospects from their existing donor databases.

investments.company: AGS Health BCP LLC (dba AGS Health)
investments.investment_type: AGS Health BCP LLC (dba AGS Health)
investments.business_description: AGS Health BCP LLC is the holding entity for AGS Health, a leading healthcare revenue cycle management (RCM) company founded in 2011 in Chennai, India. AGS Health provides end-to-end RCM services including medical coding, patient access, billing, and AI-enabled revenue cycle automation to U.S. hospitals and health systems. With over 12,000 employees and an AI platform combining automation with human-in-the-loop services, AGS supports more than 150 healthcare clients including nearly half of the 20 most prominent U.S. hospital systems.
investments.end_customers: End customers are U.S. hospitals, health systems, and physician groups that outsource revenue cycle operations to improve collections, reduce claim denials, and lower administrative costs. Large academic medical centers and integrated delivery networks are the primary buyers, relying on AGS Health's coding expertise and automation to maximize reimbursement.

investments.business_description: ClearCourse Partnership is a UK-based buy-and-build software and payments group backed by Aquiline Capital Partners, targeting small and mid-sized businesses across five divisions: Business Services, Events & Leisure, Membership Services, Payments, and Retail & Hospitality. The company has assembled over 30 software products through more than a dozen acquisitions since its founding in 2018, serving more than 20,000 customers across the UK and Ireland. Its solutions handle membership management, event booking, payments processing, and ERP-style business operations for SMEs.
investments.end_customers: ClearCourse's end customers are SMEs in sectors such as sports clubs, trade associations, leisure venues, retailers, and hospitality operators that rely on its vertical software to run daily operations including scheduling, payments, and member management. The breadth of verticals means no single industry dominates the customer base.

investments.company: SI Swan UK Bidco Limited (dba Sapiens International)
investments.investment_type: SI Swan UK Bidco Limited (dba Sapiens International)
investments.business_description: Sapiens International is an Israeli-founded, global provider of core software systems for the insurance industry, taken private by Advent International in a $2.5 billion all-cash deal that closed in December 2025. The company offers SaaS-based policy administration, claims management, billing, and digital engagement platforms covering property & casualty, life & annuities, and reinsurance segments. SI Swan UK Bidco Limited is the acquisition vehicle through which Advent completed the buyout.
investments.end_customers: Customers are insurance carriers, reinsurers, and managing general agents across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific that need modern, cloud-based core systems to replace legacy policy and claims platforms. Insurers turn to Sapiens to accelerate digital transformation and reduce the cost and risk of maintaining outdated mainframe systems.

investments.business_description: Frontgrade Technologies (formerly CAES Space Systems, rebranded by Veritas Capital in January 2023) designs and manufactures radiation-hardened (rad-hard) electronic components, ASICs, mission processing subsystems, RF amplifiers, antennas, and power management solutions for space and defense applications. The company is headquartered in Colorado Springs and serves defense primes, government agencies, and commercial satellite operators with high-reliability hardware that must survive the harsh radiation environment of orbit. In August 2023, Frontgrade acquired Aethercomm to add high-power RF solid-state power amplifiers.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are U.S. and allied-nation defense primes (e.g., Northrop Grumman, Lockheed Martin, Raytheon), intelligence agencies, NASA, and commercial satellite manufacturers that need rad-hard electronics certified for space or military environments. These buyers require ITAR-controlled, mission-critical hardware with long qualification cycles and zero-defect reliability standards.

investments.company: PPW Aero Buyer, Inc. (dba Pursuit Aerospace)
investments.investment_type: PPW Aero Buyer, Inc. (dba Pursuit Aerospace)
investments.business_description: Pursuit Aerospace is a global manufacturer of highly engineered aircraft engine components formed in February 2023 through the combination of Whitcraft Group and Paradigm Precision by Clayton, Dubilier & Rice and Greenbriar Equity Group. Headquartered in Manchester, Connecticut, the company fabricates, precision-machines, forges, and assembles components such as LPT shrouds, fan cases, combustors, turbine center frames, and oil tanks for both commercial and military aero-engine programs. Pursuit operates across multiple facilities in North America and Europe.
investments.end_customers: Customers are large aero-engine OEMs (such as GE Aerospace, Pratt & Whitney, and Rolls-Royce) and their Tier 1 suppliers who source complex structural and hot-section engine parts under long-term supply agreements. Both commercial aviation (narrow-body and wide-body jets) and military aircraft programs drive demand.

investments.investment_type: PPW Aero Buyer, Inc. (dba Pursuit Aerospace)
investments.business_description: Pursuit Aerospace is a global manufacturer of highly engineered aircraft engine components formed in February 2023 through the combination of Whitcraft Group and Paradigm Precision by Clayton, Dubilier & Rice and Greenbriar Equity Group. Headquartered in Manchester, Connecticut, the company fabricates, precision-machines, forges, and assembles components such as LPT shrouds, fan cases, combustors, turbine center frames, and oil tanks for both commercial and military aero-engine programs. Pursuit operates across multiple facilities in North America and Europe.
investments.end_customers: Customers are large aero-engine OEMs (such as GE Aerospace, Pratt & Whitney, and Rolls-Royce) and their Tier 1 suppliers who source complex structural and hot-section engine parts under long-term supply agreements. Both commercial aviation (narrow-body and wide-body jets) and military aircraft programs drive demand.

investments.business_description: VisionSafe Corporation, based in Kaneohe, Hawaii, manufactures the Emergency Vision Assurance System (EVAS), a proprietary cockpit smoke displacement device that creates a clear breathing and vision zone for pilots when dense smoke fills the cockpit during in-flight emergencies. The system holds Supplemental Type Certificates (STCs) covering over 120 aircraft models across business, cargo, passenger, and government aviation. Crescendo Capital Partners invested in the company, and The Stephens Group completed a strategic investment in April 2024.
investments.end_customers: End customers are commercial airlines, cargo carriers, business jet operators, and government aviation agencies that equip their fleets with EVAS as a cockpit safety device mandated or recommended by regulators and aviation safety standards bodies. MRO shops and aircraft OEM completion centers install the systems during new aircraft deliveries or retrofits.

investments.company: Zeppelin US Buyer Inc. (dba Global Critical Logistics)
investments.investment_type: Zeppelin US Buyer Inc. (dba Global Critical Logistics)
investments.business_description: Zeppelin US Buyer Inc. and the associated Providence Equity Partners IX-C co-investment vehicle relate to Providence Equity's acquisition of Global Critical Logistics (GCL) — a global leader in specialty logistics for live events, entertainment, and luxury goods, which traces its history to 1978 when it began serving Led Zeppelin and Uriah Heep on tour. GCL operates through brands including Rock-it Cargo, DIETL, CARS, SOS Global, and Dynamic International, with 60+ offices across 160+ countries providing end-to-end logistics including multimodal freight, event planning, warehousing, customs brokerage, and insurance. Providence acquired a majority stake in May 2025 in a deal valued at over $1 billion.
investments.end_customers: GCL's customers are major touring music acts, performing arts organizations, film and TV productions, luxury goods brands, and major sporting events that require white-glove specialty logistics for time-sensitive, high-value, or oversized cargo. Customers — ranging from stadium concert promoters to luxury auction houses — choose GCL for its specialized expertise in handling irreplaceable assets (instruments, stage equipment, art) across complex international customs and regulatory environments where standard freight carriers lack the required knowledge and care.

investments.business_description: Thrasio is one of the largest Amazon FBA (Fulfilled by Amazon) brand aggregators, founded in 2018, which acquires third-party private-label brands selling on Amazon and other e-commerce channels and then optimizes them through supply chain improvements, marketing, and product development. The company at its peak had acquired over 200 brands across categories such as home goods, health, outdoor, and pets. Thrasio filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in February 2024 and emerged from restructuring in June 2024 under new CEO Stephanie Fox.
investments.end_customers: End consumers who shop on Amazon and direct-to-consumer websites are the ultimate buyers of Thrasio's portfolio of everyday consumer goods. Thrasio's own direct customers are the original brand founders who sell their FBA businesses to Thrasio in exchange for cash consideration.

investments.business_description: Elemica is a cloud-based supply chain network and SaaS platform originally founded in 2000 by a consortium of leading chemical companies, acquired by Eurazeo in 2019 for approximately $390 million. The platform connects buyers, sellers, and logistics providers in the process industries—primarily chemicals, life sciences, and related sectors—enabling automated order-to-cash and procure-to-pay transactions. The network facilitates over $1 trillion in annual commerce across more than 900 customers, including 39 of the top 100 global chemical companies.
investments.end_customers: Customers are chemical companies, specialty materials manufacturers, logistics service providers, and distributors that need a neutral, many-to-many digital supply network to automate transactions with their trading partners. Large chemical producers use Elemica to eliminate manual EDI integrations and achieve end-to-end visibility into orders, shipments, and invoices across complex global supply chains.

investments.business_description: 3SI Security Systems, founded in 1971 and backed by Resilience Capital Partners, is the world's leading provider of asset protection and criminal apprehension systems for financial institutions, deploying GPS tracking devices, dye packs (SecurityPac), and integrated video management solutions. When a bank robbery occurs, 3SI's SecurityPac releases red smoke and permanent dye, while its ESP (Electronic Satellite Pursuit) GPS tracker silently transmits the thief's precise location to law enforcement in real time. The company serves a large installed base of banks, credit unions, and retail financial services locations across North America.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are banks, credit unions, and financial institution security teams that embed 3SI systems in cash drawers, ATMs, and cash transport to deter robbery and aid recovery of stolen funds. Law enforcement agencies are operational partners who act on 3SI's GPS tracking data to apprehend suspects and recover stolen cash.

investments.company: Buckeye Acquiror LLC (dba Superior Environmental Solutions)
investments.investment_type: Buckeye Acquiror LLC (dba Superior Environmental Solutions)
investments.business_description: Superior Environmental Solutions (SES), headquartered in Cincinnati, Ohio, and backed by Pfingsten Partners, provides industrial cleaning, vacuum truck services, hydro-blasting, hazardous waste handling, emergency environmental response, and remediation services to industrial customers. The company operates from more than 44 facilities across the Midwest, Southeast, and beyond after completing several acquisitions, including American Remediation & Environmental in 2024. SES serves the chemical, refining, power, food and beverage, and heavy industrial end markets.
investments.end_customers: Customers are industrial plant operators—refineries, chemical plants, power generators, food processors, and manufacturers—that outsource specialized industrial cleaning and environmental compliance services they cannot safely or economically perform with in-house labor. Emergency response capabilities are critical for customers managing hazardous spills or regulatory obligations under environmental law.

investments.business_description: Edko LLC, backed by Incline Equity Partners, is a vegetation management services company headquartered in Bossier City, Louisiana, providing herbicide-based treatment programs to utilities, municipalities, pipeline operators, and other infrastructure rights-of-way owners. The company operates 14+ branch locations serving 25 states, helping clients plan and implement environmentally responsible herbicide programs to control invasive vegetation along power lines, pipelines, and road corridors. Edko has expanded through add-on acquisitions including BCC, Inc., a land reclamation and weed control firm.
investments.end_customers: Customers are electric utilities, gas pipeline companies, municipalities, and railroad operators that must legally maintain clear rights-of-way to prevent vegetation from damaging infrastructure or causing outages. Herbicide-based vegetation management is more cost-effective and durable than mechanical cutting alone, driving repeat outsourced service demand.

investments.business_description: ES Integrated (operating as EnviroSmart LLC, backed by Kinderhook Industries) is an environmental and industrial services company providing industrial cleaning, environmental remediation, earthwork, piping, remedial construction, and facilities operations and maintenance support to federal and commercial clients. The company is a woman-owned small business headquartered in Charleston with 200+ associates offering integrated solutions for complex environmental, construction, and industrial challenges. Its work includes hazardous waste remediation, industrial cleaning, and natural resource management.
investments.end_customers: Customers span federal government agencies (DoD, EPA) and commercial industrial operators that need specialized environmental services for contaminated site cleanup, operational facility maintenance, and industrial cleaning. Government contracts provide a stable base, while commercial clients in energy, manufacturing, and construction sectors drive additional demand.

investments.business_description: Frontline Road Safety is the largest provider of pavement marking and roadway safety services in the U.S., acquired by Bain Capital in March 2025 from The Sterling Group. Headquartered in Denver with 50-plus locations nationwide, Frontline provides highway and airport line striping, thermoplastic and epoxy pavement markings, traffic control management, safety signage, and work zone services. Sterling built the platform from 2020 through 19 acquisitions before selling to Bain Capital.
investments.end_customers: State and local transportation departments (DOTs), municipalities, airport operators, and private contractors engaged in road construction and maintenance projects that require compliant pavement markings and traffic control services under federal, state, and local highway safety standards.

investments.business_description: Kene Acquisition and Kene Holdings are the equity investment holding vehicles through which Kohlberg & Company owns a capital goods manufacturing or industrial services business. Kohlberg focuses on manufacturing and services sectors in the middle market, and 'Kene' is a proprietary deal name. Public disclosure about the specific underlying operating business is limited, though the equity classification and Kohlberg's investment profile suggest a niche industrial manufacturer or specialty distributor.
investments.end_customers: Based on Kohlberg's typical portfolio focus (manufacturing, industrial services), end customers are likely industrial OEMs, distributors, or commercial operators requiring specialty manufactured goods or industrial services.

investments.company: Legends Hospitality Holding Company, LLC (fka ASM Buyer, Inc.)
investments.investment_type: Legends Hospitality Holding Company, LLC (fka ASM Buyer, Inc.)
investments.business_description: Legends Global (formerly Legends Hospitality) is the world's largest integrated venue services and sports business solutions company, owned by Sixth Street Partners, the New York Yankees, and the Dallas Cowboys. Following the 2024 acquisition of ASM Global—manager of over 300 arenas, stadiums, convention centers, and theaters worldwide (including NRG Stadium, OVO Arena Wembley, and Accor Stadium Sydney)—the combined entity operates over 450 venues, hosts 20,000 events annually, and entertains 165 million guests. Its services span premium hospitality and food/beverage, planning and development consulting, global sales, and fan engagement technology.
investments.end_customers: Sports franchises, concert promoters, convention organizers, and municipal venue owners engage Legends Global to manage and operate their facilities, including food service, premium experiences, and corporate hospitality. Ultimately, sports fans, concertgoers, and convention attendees are the end consumers of Legends' services at each venue.

investments.business_description: Sweep Purchaser LLC is the acquisition vehicle used by Warburg Pincus to invest in Sweeping Corporation of America (SCA), the largest outsourced provider of street and parking lot sweeping services in the United States. Based in Cleveland, Ohio, SCA operates 35+ locations with over 1,000 employees across the eastern half of the U.S., providing street sweeping, highway sweeping, and jet-vac services. Warburg Pincus acquired SCA from Soundcore Capital Partners in November 2020 and has supported the company's national growth through add-on acquisitions.
investments.end_customers: Customers are municipalities, transportation departments, real estate developers, and property managers (parking garages, shopping centers, industrial parks) that outsource routine street and lot sweeping to maintain cleanliness, comply with stormwater runoff regulations, and preserve pavement surfaces. Municipal governments represent the largest and most recurring revenue source.

investments.business_description: Triumvirate Environmental, backed by Berkshire Partners at a $1.8 billion valuation (as of early 2025), is a leading provider of hazardous and non-hazardous environmental services headquartered in Massachusetts. The company serves life sciences, healthcare, education, and industrial customers with on-site environmental support, hazardous and regulated waste handling and disposal, compliance consulting, and strategic advisory services across approximately 50 locations in North America. Founded in 1988 as a three-person Boston operation, Triumvirate has grown to approximately $500 million in annual revenue.
investments.end_customers: Customers are hospitals, pharmaceutical and biotech companies, universities, research institutions, and industrial manufacturers that generate regulated or hazardous waste and need compliant disposal, on-site safety support, and environmental compliance expertise. Life sciences organizations are particularly large customers given the volume and variety of chemical and biological waste they generate.

investments.business_description: USA DeBusk, headquartered in Deer Park, Texas, and acquired by H.I.G. Capital in May 2024, is a leading industrial cleaning and infrastructure services company providing hydro-blasting, tube cleaning, tube inspection (nondestructive testing), vacuum services, and specialty industrial cleaning to the chemical, renewable fuels, refining, and power generation industries. The company operates from 39 offices with over 2,500 full-time employees and is known for integrating inspection and advanced tube cleaning as a single-source service offering. CEO Andrew DeBusk founded the company and continued to lead it post-acquisition.
investments.end_customers: Customers are refineries, petrochemical plants, power plants, and renewable fuel producers that rely on USA DeBusk for scheduled and unscheduled maintenance cleaning of heat exchangers, boilers, condensers, and process vessels to maintain equipment efficiency and regulatory compliance. Turnaround maintenance cycles at these facilities drive lumpy but large recurring demand.

investments.company: Valet Waste Holdings, Inc. (dba Valet Living)
investments.investment_type: Valet Waste Holdings, Inc. (dba Valet Living)
investments.business_description: Valet Living, acquired by GI Partners in November 2020, is the largest nationally recognized full-service amenities provider to the multifamily apartment industry in the United States, serving 1.6 million apartment homes across 40 states with more than 415 million service events annually. The company pioneered the doorstep valet trash and recycling collection model and has expanded into turns (apartment make-ready), pet solutions, maintenance, and app-enabled on-demand resident services such as housekeeping and dog walking. Valet Living is headquartered in Tampa, Florida.
investments.end_customers: Customers are multifamily property management companies and apartment REITs that contract Valet Living to provide branded resident amenities that improve tenant satisfaction and retention. Property owners pay for the service as a revenue-generating amenity—residents often pay a monthly fee—making Valet Living's model attractive to owners seeking differentiation in competitive rental markets.

investments.company: VRC Companies, LLC (dba Vital Records Control)
investments.investment_type: VRC Companies, LLC (dba Vital Records Control)
investments.business_description: VRC Companies is the parent of Vital Records Control (VRC), a records management and information governance company providing physical document storage, secure shredding, digital document scanning, and information lifecycle management services to businesses across multiple industries. VRC competes with Iron Mountain and Recall in the records management market, primarily serving mid-market clients with physical records storage and destruction needs. The company manages secure storage of paper documents, x-rays, and other physical media on behalf of its customers.
investments.end_customers: End customers are healthcare organizations, law firms, financial institutions, corporations, and government agencies that generate and retain significant volumes of physical documents required for compliance or business operations. These organizations outsource records storage and management to VRC to reduce internal space costs, ensure secure controlled access, and comply with document retention and destruction policies.

investments.company: Wildcat Solutions Holdings, LLC (dba O6 Environmental)
investments.investment_type: Wildcat Solutions Holdings, LLC (dba O6 Environmental)
investments.business_description: O6 Environmental (operating under Wildcat Solutions Holdings, LLC, backed by Kinderhook Industries) is an environmental remediation and industrial services company providing environmental remediation, industrial cleaning, excavation, and related solutions to industrial and commercial clients. The company has over 20 years of experience delivering tailored environmental services with a strong commitment to sustainability and safe remediation practices. Kinderhook is a prolific acquirer in the environmental and industrial services sector with over 500 investments completed since inception.
investments.end_customers: Customers are industrial companies, real estate developers, and government agencies managing contaminated sites or industrial facilities that require remediation, excavation, or cleaning services to comply with environmental regulations or prepare properties for reuse. Repeat service relationships are common as industrial operators conduct recurring scheduled maintenance alongside episodic remediation projects.

investments.business_description: ATX Networks, acquired by H.I.G. Capital in 2015, is a designer and manufacturer of radio frequency (RF) management, digital video processing, and broadband access equipment used by cable operators, telecom companies, and private video networks. The company's product portfolio spans cable headend equipment, RF amplifiers, transcoding and encoding hardware, bulk video transition gateways, and content streaming products that enable cable operators to increase bandwidth and deliver video and data services to end subscribers. In January 2017, ATX acquired Pico Digital to expand its digital video product line.
investments.end_customers: Customers are cable MSOs (multiple system operators), telecom companies, A/V integrators, and private network operators that deploy ATX's equipment in their headend facilities to manage and distribute video and broadband signals to millions of subscribers. Demand is driven by cable operators' ongoing network upgrade cycles to increase capacity for internet and streaming video services.

investments.company: Geo TopCo Corporation (fka Geotechnical Merger Sub, Inc.)
investments.investment_type: Geo TopCo Corporation (fka Geotechnical Merger Sub, Inc.)
investments.business_description: Geo TopCo Corporation is a private equity-backed geotechnical and materials testing services platform backed by Trilantic North America, formed through the combination of geotechnical engineering and subsurface investigation businesses operating in the construction and infrastructure testing sector. The company provides geotechnical site investigation, soil and materials testing, construction quality assurance, and environmental consulting services to support infrastructure and real estate development projects. Trilantic North America focuses on founder-led middle-market businesses in business and consumer services.
investments.end_customers: Customers are real estate developers, civil engineering firms, construction contractors, municipalities, and government agencies that require independent third-party geotechnical testing and materials inspection to verify soil conditions, structural integrity, and code compliance before and during construction projects. Testing services are often mandated by lenders, permitting authorities, or engineering standards.

investments.business_description: SimPRO (operating under Sonar Acquisitionco, Inc., backed by K1 Investment Management with a $350+ million investment in late 2021) is a global field service management (FSM) software platform serving trade contractors in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, security, and other skilled trades sectors. The platform covers job quoting, scheduling and dispatch, inventory management, project tracking, invoicing, and integrations with accounting software, supporting both small businesses and large enterprise contractors. SimPRO has expanded through acquisitions of ClockShark (time-tracking), AroFlo (Australian job management), and BigChange (UK fleet and mobile workforce management).
investments.end_customers: Customers are trade contractors—HVAC, plumbing, electrical, fire protection, and security installation companies—from sole operators to large enterprise service businesses that use SimPRO to manage their entire job lifecycle digitally instead of relying on paper-based workflows. The software helps contractors reduce administrative overhead, improve technician utilization, and bill faster.

investments.business_description: SimPRO (operating under Sonar Acquisitionco, Inc., backed by K1 Investment Management with a $350+ million investment in late 2021) is a global field service management (FSM) software platform serving trade contractors in HVAC, plumbing, electrical, security, and other skilled trades sectors. The platform covers job quoting, scheduling and dispatch, inventory management, project tracking, invoicing, and integrations with accounting software, supporting both small businesses and large enterprise contractors. SimPRO has expanded through acquisitions of ClockShark (time-tracking), AroFlo (Australian job management), and BigChange (UK fleet and mobile workforce management).
investments.end_customers: Customers are trade contractors—HVAC, plumbing, electrical, fire protection, and security installation companies—from sole operators to large enterprise service businesses that use SimPRO to manage their entire job lifecycle digitally instead of relying on paper-based workflows. The software helps contractors reduce administrative overhead, improve technician utilization, and bill faster.

investments.business_description: Superman Holdings, LLC (doing business as Foundation Software) is the Thoma Bravo-backed holding entity for Foundation Software, the leading provider of construction accounting, payroll, and ERP software for specialty subcontractors in the United States, acquired by Thoma Bravo in 2020. Foundation's FOUNDATION® software — branded as "America's #1 construction accounting software" — serves electrical, plumbing, roofing, HVAC, and other trade contractors with integrated modules for job cost accounting, payroll (including union and certified payroll), bidding and estimating, project management, mobile field apps, equipment tracking, and HR compliance. Founded in Ohio in 1985 by Fred Ode (who retained a minority stake post-acquisition), the company has served specialty subcontractors for over 35 years.
investments.end_customers: Foundation Software's customers are small-to-midsized specialty subcontracting businesses — electricians, plumbers, roofers, HVAC contractors, and other trade contractors — that need construction-specific accounting and payroll software that handles the unique complexities of the construction industry, including job cost tracking, AIA billing, certified payroll, and union reporting. Contractors' office managers, controllers, and payroll administrators rely on FOUNDATION as their primary ERP and payroll system, with switching costs that are very high given the embedded nature of job costing and historical data. The company's long tenure gives it strong brand loyalty among trade contractors, particularly in the Northeast and Midwest where union reporting requirements are more complex.

investments.company: Blast Bidco Inc. (dba Bazooka Candy Brands)
investments.investment_type: Blast Bidco Inc. (dba Bazooka Candy Brands)
investments.business_description: Bazooka Candy Brands (operating under Blast Bidco Inc., acquired by Apax Partners in October 2023 for approximately $700 million) is a leading non-chocolate confectionery company whose portfolio includes Ring Pop, Push Pop, Baby Bottle Pop, Juicy Drop, and Bazooka Bubble Gum. The company markets its brands as "Edible Entertainment," featuring interactive and novelty candy formats spanning lollipops, gummies, chewy candy, gum, and chocolate. Bazooka's U.S. retail sales growth significantly outpaced the broader confectionery category in the period leading up to the acquisition.
investments.end_customers: End consumers are primarily children and teens attracted to the novelty and interactive formats of Bazooka's candy brands. Retail customers include mass merchandisers, drug chains, convenience stores, and grocery retailers in the U.S. and internationally that stock the confectionery brands in candy aisles and checkout lanes.

investments.company: Oliver Packaging and Equipment Company, LLC (fka Buffalo Merger Sub, LLC)
investments.investment_type: Oliver Packaging and Equipment Company, LLC (fka Buffalo Merger Sub, LLC)
investments.business_description: Oliver Packaging and Equipment Company (founded in 1890, headquartered in Walker, Michigan, acquired by Monomoy Capital Partners in November 2024) is a manufacturer of heat seal food packaging systems, bakery slicing and wrapping equipment, fiber-based meal trays, recyclable CPET plastic meal trays and film, and aftermarket parts and service for its installed equipment base. The company manufactures all its heat sealers and slicers at its Walker, Michigan headquarters. Oliver serves food processors, bakeries, and institutional food service operators.
investments.end_customers: Customers are food manufacturers, commercial bakeries, contract food packagers, healthcare food service operations, and school lunch programs that use Oliver's packaging machinery and trays to portion, seal, and package prepared foods and baked goods for retail and institutional distribution. The large installed base of Oliver equipment drives recurring aftermarket parts and service revenue.

investments.business_description: Precision Concepts International (PCI), originally backed by ONCAP (Onex Corporation's lower mid-market PE platform) since 2017 and subsequently sold to The Sterling Group in July 2025, is a designer and manufacturer of specialty rigid consumer packaging solutions including sticks, jars, vials, closures, spouts, bottles, and tubes, serving personal care, household, food and beverage, and nutraceutical end markets. The company is headquartered in Huntersville, North Carolina, and has grown through six strategic acquisitions under ONCAP ownership. ONCAP retained a minority position post-sale.
investments.end_customers: Customers are branded consumer goods companies in personal care (cosmetics, skincare), household products, food and beverage, and dietary supplements that source specialty rigid packaging components from PCI for their branded products. Packaging specifications are often custom-designed per client brand, making PCI an integrated development and manufacturing partner rather than a commodity supplier.

investments.business_description: A Place For Mom, backed by General Atlantic and Silver Lake (investment in 2017, additional $175 million round in 2022 valuing the company at over $1 billion), is North America's largest senior care referral and advisory service, connecting families with assisted living, memory care, independent living, and home care options through a network of over 500 Senior Living Advisors. The service is free to families; A Place For Mom earns placement fees from its network of partner senior living communities and care providers. The company is headquartered in New York.
investments.end_customers: Families seeking care options for aging parents or relatives are the primary users of A Place For Mom's advisory service. Senior living communities—assisted living facilities, memory care communities, and home care agencies—are the paying customers that subscribe to the referral network and pay placement fees when A Place For Mom directs a resident to their community.

investments.company: ABC Investment Holdco Inc. (dba ABC Plumbing)
investments.investment_type: ABC Investment Holdco Inc. (dba ABC Plumbing)
investments.business_description: ABC Plumbing (operating under ABC Investment Holdco Inc., backed by Roark Capital Group) is a home services platform providing residential and commercial plumbing, HVAC, electrical, and sewer services in the Chicago metropolitan area and expanding to Florida, with over 70 years of operating history. The company also operates a partnership acquisition program (partners.4abc.com) through which it acquires and integrates independent home service businesses to accelerate geographic expansion. Roark Capital focuses on multi-location, franchise-like business models in consumer services.
investments.end_customers: Customers are homeowners and commercial property owners in the Midwest and Southeast who need plumbing, heating, cooling, and electrical repair and installation services, including 24/7 emergency response. The recurring nature of home system maintenance and the high urgency of emergency plumbing and HVAC failures create a stable and repeat demand profile.

investments.business_description: SavATree (operating under CI (Quercus) Intermediate Holdings, LLC, acquired by Apax Partners from CI Capital in Q4 2021) is a professional tree care, shrub care, and lawn care services company headquartered in Bedford Hills, New York. Under CI Capital's ownership, the company tripled revenue and grew from 28 branches in 12 states to 58+ branches serving 27 states through a combination of organic growth and 27 add-on acquisitions. SavATree emphasizes an environmentally sensible approach and serves residential, commercial, and government properties.
investments.end_customers: Customers are homeowners, commercial property owners, municipalities, and golf courses that engage SavATree for certified arborist-led tree and shrub care, lawn fertilization, pest and disease management, and related property health services. Recurring seasonal maintenance programs represent the largest portion of revenue, as clients rely on SavATree year-round to maintain the health and appearance of trees and turf.

investments.business_description: CST Holding Company is the holding entity for The Service Companies, the nation's premier provider of managed housekeeping, public area cleaning, food and beverage staffing, and specialty cleaning services for the hospitality and gaming industries. The Service Companies serves over 750 hotels, casinos, resorts, arenas, stadiums, hospitals, and universities across 33 states, Puerto Rico, and the Bahamas under outsourced management contracts. Services include turnkey housekeeping, kitchen and steward cleaning, laundry, chandelier and window cleaning, and trained F&B staffing.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are hotel operators, casino resort owners, arena and stadium managers, university facilities teams, and corporate cafeteria operators who outsource non-core hospitality labor to The Service Companies to improve quality, reduce HR complexity, and better manage variable labor costs during seasonal and event-driven demand swings.

investments.company: Heartland Home Services, Inc. (fka Helios Buyer, Inc.)
investments.investment_type: Heartland Home Services, Inc. (fka Helios Buyer, Inc.)
investments.business_description: Heartland Home Services is a leading technology-enabled platform of residential HVAC, plumbing, and electrical home service companies, headquartered in Grand Rapids, Michigan. The company operates through 35+ local brands across the Midwest and generates approximately $1 billion in annual revenue. Backed by TJC (The Jordan Company), Heartland grows through acquisitions of established regional HVAC, plumbing, and electrical contractors, preserving local brand identity while providing centralized operational support.
investments.end_customers: Heartland serves residential homeowners who need heating, cooling, plumbing, or electrical repair, maintenance, and replacement services. Customers are primarily Midwestern homeowners seeking reliable, locally-trusted service providers for urgent or planned home mechanical system needs.

investments.company: Pacvue Intermediate LLC (fka Assembly Intermediate LLC)
investments.investment_type: Pacvue Intermediate LLC (fka Assembly Intermediate LLC)
investments.business_description: Pacvue (formerly operating as Assembly, backed by Advent International which led a $1 billion+ valuation round in 2021) is a commerce acceleration software platform that enables brands, retailers, and agencies to manage, optimize, and measure advertising, sales, and retail media programs across Amazon, Walmart, Instacart, and other major e-commerce marketplaces. The platform combines retail media activation, marketplace operations, and analytics into a unified suite; Assembly rebranded to Pacvue after acquiring the Pacvue platform. The company also owns Helium 10, a seller intelligence tool.
investments.end_customers: Customers are consumer brands, their agencies, and marketplace sellers of all sizes that invest in retail media advertising (sponsored products, display, video) across Amazon and other digital marketplaces and need a technology platform to manage campaign bidding, budgets, reporting, and competitive intelligence at scale. Enterprise consumer packaged goods companies are a key segment alongside mid-market brands seeking to grow marketplace share.

investments.company: Pacvue Intermediate LLC (fka Assembly Intermediate LLC)
investments.investment_type: Pacvue Intermediate LLC (fka Assembly Intermediate LLC)
investments.business_description: Pacvue (formerly operating as Assembly, backed by Advent International which led a $1 billion+ valuation round in 2021) is a commerce acceleration software platform that enables brands, retailers, and agencies to manage, optimize, and measure advertising, sales, and retail media programs across Amazon, Walmart, Instacart, and other major e-commerce marketplaces. The platform combines retail media activation, marketplace operations, and analytics into a unified suite; Assembly rebranded to Pacvue after acquiring the Pacvue platform. The company also owns Helium 10, a seller intelligence tool.
investments.end_customers: Customers are consumer brands, their agencies, and marketplace sellers of all sizes that invest in retail media advertising (sponsored products, display, video) across Amazon and other digital marketplaces and need a technology platform to manage campaign bidding, budgets, reporting, and competitive intelligence at scale. Enterprise consumer packaged goods companies are a key segment alongside mid-market brands seeking to grow marketplace share.

investments.business_description: Southeast Mechanical (SEM), backed by Palladin Consumer Retail Partners since July 2022, is an HVAC, plumbing, and electrical services platform built through acquisitions of established local contractors across North Carolina, South Carolina, and Georgia. Founded in 2020 and headquartered in Winston-Salem, North Carolina, the company operates through multiple regional brands including Triangle Contractors, Warren-Hay Mechanical, Airmakers Heating & Cooling, and Logan Heating & Air, serving over 11,000 residential and commercial customers with 300+ professionals. The platform has completed several tuck-in acquisitions including All Ways Heating and Air, Total Services HVAC, Climate Design Home Services, and Carolina Comfort.
investments.end_customers: Customers are homeowners and light commercial property owners in the Carolinas and Georgia who need HVAC installation, repair, and seasonal maintenance, along with plumbing and electrical services. Recurring maintenance agreements and equipment replacement cycles drive predictable revenue, while emergency HVAC failures in extreme weather provide high-urgency demand.

investments.business_description: Splash Car Wash, founded in 1981 and formerly backed by Palladin Consumer Retail Partners (sold to AEA Investors in April 2025), is a premier car wash operator with 65+ locations across Connecticut, Massachusetts, New York, and Vermont offering full-service, hand-wash, express tunnel, and detailing services. The company has built out its express car wash footprint through greenfield openings and acquisitions and operates a membership-based model alongside pay-per-wash options. Palladin completed six add-on acquisitions during its ownership, and AEA acquired the majority position for continued growth.
investments.end_customers: End customers are vehicle owners in the Northeast U.S. who use Splash for routine exterior washing, interior cleaning, and detailing services. Membership programs that provide unlimited monthly washes are increasingly the primary revenue driver, appealing to frequent users who value convenience and cost predictability for vehicle maintenance.

investments.business_description: Spotless Brands, backed by Access Holdings since 2020, is a super-regional express car wash platform operating 220+ locations across 13 states (including Arizona, Colorado, Maryland, Virginia, D.C., Ohio, Tennessee, and others) under brands including Flagship Carwash, Cobblestone Auto Spa, Cobblestone Car Wash, Ultimate Shine Car Wash, and Okie Express Auto Wash. The company operates primarily express tunnel car washes with monthly membership models and has grown rapidly through both acquisitions and greenfield development. A recent acquisition of Pete's Express Car Wash (six Philadelphia-area sites) extended the company into the Pennsylvania market.
investments.end_customers: End customers are vehicle owners seeking affordable, fast, express car wash services, particularly unlimited monthly membership plans that incentivize frequent visits. Property of the membership model means Spotless generates recurring monthly subscription revenue from a large installed base of member vehicles, smoothing out weather-driven variability in single-wash demand.

investments.company: Sunshine Cadence HoldCo, LLC (dba Cadence Education)
investments.investment_type: Sunshine Cadence HoldCo, LLC (dba Cadence Education)
investments.business_description: Cadence Education (operating under Sunshine Cadence HoldCo, LLC, acquired by Apax Partners from Morgan Stanley Capital Partners in March 2020) is a leading provider of early childhood education, operating over 300 private preschools across the U.S. under more than 40 brands including its flagship Cadence Academy. The company serves children from six weeks through 12 years of age with proprietary curriculum developed by early childhood education experts across its national network of campuses. With over 27 years in the business, Cadence combines premium early learning programs with large-scale multi-site operations.
investments.end_customers: Customers are working parents who need full-day or part-time childcare and early education programs for infants, toddlers, and preschool-age children. Parents choose Cadence for the combination of developmental curriculum, experienced caregivers, and the convenience of locations near their homes or workplaces, paying tuition directly on a monthly basis.

investments.business_description: VASA Fitness, backed by Silver Oak Services Partners (initial recapitalization in November 2014), is a high-value, low-price (HVLP) fitness club chain operating 70+ locations across Utah, Colorado, Arizona, Oklahoma, Nebraska, Indiana, Illinois, and Wisconsin, making it the largest gym operator in the Utah and Colorado markets. The company offers large-format clubs (full-service locations with pools, group fitness, personal training, basketball, and childcare) and smaller express clubs at affordable monthly membership prices starting at $14.99/month. VASA acquired 17 Gold's Gym locations in 2014 as part of its expansion strategy.
investments.end_customers: Members are value-conscious fitness consumers, primarily in the Mountain West and Midwest, who want access to premium amenities (pools, childcare, group fitness studios, basketball courts) at price points well below boutique fitness studios. The affordable monthly membership model attracts a broad demographic spanning students, families, and working adults seeking accessible fitness in their local communities.

investments.business_description: Trystar is a manufacturer of critical electrical power distribution and backup power solutions, producing cable assemblies, portable and permanent power distribution equipment, automatic transfer switches, and generator docking stations. Founded in 1991 and headquartered in Faribault, Minnesota, the company was acquired by Blackstone in 2024. Trystar's equipment is purpose-built for temporary and emergency power applications across data centers, healthcare facilities, utilities, industrial sites, and commercial buildings.
investments.end_customers: Customers include hospitals, data centers, utilities, event production companies, entertainment venues, educational institutions, and government agencies that require reliable temporary or backup power. Trystar sells both directly and through distribution, targeting end users who need dependable power during outages, construction, or special events.

investments.company: Pearl Acquisition Buyer, Inc. (dba Alliance Technical Group)
investments.investment_type: Pearl Acquisition Buyer, Inc. (dba Alliance Technical Group)
investments.business_description: Alliance Technical Group is a leading full-service environmental compliance and testing firm headquartered in Decatur, Alabama, originally founded in 2000 as a stack testing company. The company operates across seven service lines: stack testing, leak detection and repair (LDAR), continuous emissions monitoring systems (CEMS), mechanical field services, analytical laboratory services, ambient air monitoring, and environmental consulting. With over 27 locations and roughly 1,000–5,000 employees, it has grown into one of the largest environmental services providers in North America.
investments.end_customers: Alliance serves industrial operators including refineries, chemical plants, power generators, paper mills, and manufacturing facilities that must demonstrate regulatory compliance with EPA and state environmental standards. Clients rely on Alliance to conduct mandatory emissions testing, monitor ongoing compliance, and avoid costly regulatory penalties.

investments.company: Chess.com, LLC (fka Checkmate Finance Merger Sub, LLC)
investments.investment_type: Chess.com, LLC (fka Checkmate Finance Merger Sub, LLC)
investments.business_description: Chess.com is the world's largest online chess platform, launched in 2007, with over 75 million registered members playing more than 10 million games daily. The platform provides online chess gameplay, puzzles, game analysis, grandmaster lessons, and coaching tools in both free and premium subscription tiers. General Atlantic made a growth equity investment in January 2022 to support continued scaling of the platform and brand.
investments.end_customers: End users range from casual beginners to competitive club and tournament players seeking improvement, analysis tools, and a community. Chess.com monetizes primarily through premium memberships and serves chess enthusiasts globally, with its audience surging during the COVID-19 pandemic and the popularity boost from the Netflix series 'The Queen's Gambit.'

investments.business_description: Streamland Media is a facility-based, technology-enabled post-production services company headquartered in Los Angeles, acquired by Trive Capital in 2018. The company provides picture finishing, color grading, sound design and editing, visual effects (VFX), dailies processing, and marketing content services for feature films, episodic television, gaming, and interactive media. It operates through well-known industry brands including Picture Shop, Ghost VFX, Formosa Group, and Picture Head, with facilities across Los Angeles, Vancouver, Copenhagen, and the United Kingdom.
investments.end_customers: Streamland's clients are major film studios, streaming platforms (Netflix, Disney+, Amazon), independent production companies, and video game publishers that require high-quality post-production finishing before content release. These clients depend on Streamland's specialized facilities and technical talent for the final creative and technical stages of content production.

investments.company: Admiral Buyer, Inc. (dba Fidelity Payment Services)
investments.investment_type: Admiral Buyer, Inc. (dba Fidelity Payment Services)
investments.business_description: Fidelity Payment Services is a large independent payment processing company founded in 1996, built around its proprietary Cardknox omni-channel payment technology platform. Cardknox is a cloud-native solution enabling merchants to accept credit, debit, EMV chip, ACH, and mobile wallet payments across in-store, online, mobile, and self-service channels. PSG recapitalized the company in 2022, at which point the platform processed over $15 billion in annual card volume across more than 22,000 merchants.
investments.end_customers: Merchants across grocery, healthcare, restaurants, charitable organizations, education, entertainment, retail, and travel rely on Fidelity Payment Services for payment processing infrastructure. The company targets small and mid-size businesses that need a flexible, developer-friendly payment platform with broad payment type support.

investments.investment_type: Admiral Buyer, Inc. (dba Fidelity Payment Services)
investments.business_description: Fidelity Payment Services is a large independent payment processing company founded in 1996, built around its proprietary Cardknox omni-channel payment technology platform. Cardknox is a cloud-native solution enabling merchants to accept credit, debit, EMV chip, ACH, and mobile wallet payments across in-store, online, mobile, and self-service channels. PSG recapitalized the company in 2022, at which point the platform processed over $15 billion in annual card volume across more than 22,000 merchants.
investments.end_customers: Merchants across grocery, healthcare, restaurants, charitable organizations, education, entertainment, retail, and travel rely on Fidelity Payment Services for payment processing infrastructure. The company targets small and mid-size businesses that need a flexible, developer-friendly payment platform with broad payment type support.

investments.business_description: Aria Systems is a San Francisco-based enterprise SaaS company providing a cloud billing and monetization platform designed for companies with complex subscription, usage-based, and recurring revenue models. Founded in 2002, the Aria Billing Cloud platform manages customer lifecycle, pricing configurations, revenue recognition, and integrations with ERP and CRM systems. Aria is recognized as a leader in telecom billing and serves industries including communications, media, software, and industrial IoT.
investments.end_customers: Aria's customers are large enterprises in communications, media and publishing, SaaS, and technology—companies like telecoms and streaming services—that need a scalable billing engine capable of supporting millions of subscribers and varied pricing models. Aria is a billing partner of choice for platforms like ServiceNow and operates alongside Salesforce as a preferred partner for lead-to-care workflows.

investments.business_description: Ren (formerly known in the philanthropic technology space as Renaissance) is the leading independent philanthropic solutions provider in North America, founded in 1987 and headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana. The company provides expertise, standards, and technology to manage charitable giving programs including donor-advised funds, charitable remainder trusts, pooled income funds, endowments, and private foundations on behalf of financial institutions and nonprofit organizations. Bain Capital Tech Opportunities led a growth investment in the company in 2023.
investments.end_customers: Ren's clients are financial services firms, community foundations, and non-profit organizations that outsource the administration of complex charitable giving vehicles to specialists. These institutions rely on Ren's compliance expertise and operational infrastructure to manage the fiduciary and technical requirements of running philanthropic programs at scale.

investments.business_description: Kydia, operating also as BeyondMenu.com, is an online ordering and digital marketing platform for independent and chain restaurants, founded in 2004 and headquartered in Rosemont, Illinois. The company provides restaurants with commission-based online ordering systems, website design, and search engine optimization services, enabling them to accept and manage digital orders directly. It is a small, niche technology-enabled services business focused on the food service vertical.
investments.end_customers: Kydia's customers are independent restaurant operators and small restaurant chains looking for affordable tools to establish an online ordering presence and reduce dependence on large third-party delivery aggregators. Restaurants pay a commission on orders processed through the BeyondMenu platform rather than high flat fees.

investments.business_description: Celero Commerce is a full-service electronic payments and business management software provider headquartered in Nashville, Tennessee, serving small and medium-sized businesses across North America. The company offers payment processing, point-of-sale hardware and software, business intelligence, and data analytics through an integrated platform, and processes over $28 billion in annual card volume. Celero has grown aggressively through acquisitions including Everest Payment Solutions, OMEGA Technology, Community Bankers Merchant Services, and Precision Payments.
investments.end_customers: Celero's customers are small and mid-size merchants across diverse sectors including retail, hospitality, and service businesses that require affordable, integrated payment acceptance and business software. The company distributes heavily through financial institution partnerships, offering payment services to community banks and credit unions that resell to their business customers.

investments.business_description: Computer Services, Inc. (CSI) is a fintech and regtech software and services company serving community and mid-size banks, credit unions, and other financial institutions. Centerbridge Partners and Bridgeport Partners took CSI private in 2022 for approximately $1.6 billion. CSI provides core banking systems, digital banking platforms, cybersecurity, compliance management, and payment processing to roughly 3,000 financial institutions across the U.S. The company is headquartered in Paducah, Kentucky.
investments.end_customers: Customers are community banks, savings institutions, and credit unions that rely on CSI's core banking software and adjacent services for deposit processing, loan origination, online banking, and regulatory compliance reporting. CSI's bank-specific software is deeply integrated into customer operations, making it a classic sticky, recurring-revenue business.

investments.business_description: CoreTrust is a group purchasing organization (GPO) that leverages the aggregated buying power of its members—primarily large corporations and private equity portfolio companies—to negotiate favorable pricing and terms with vendors for a wide range of non-clinical goods and services such as office supplies, technology, logistics, and travel. The company originated as a program within HCA Healthcare's HealthTrust subsidiary in 2006 before Blackstone acquired a majority stake in 2022, with HealthTrust retaining a minority interest. CoreTrust has historically saved Blackstone's portfolio companies nearly $200 million through contract purchasing programs.
investments.end_customers: CoreTrust's members are large corporations and private equity-backed portfolio companies seeking to reduce procurement costs across their operations. PE firms including Blackstone, KKR, and Onex have directed hundreds of portfolio companies to the platform, making CoreTrust a strategic back-office resource for PE-owned businesses.

investments.business_description: Fullsteam is a holding company and payments platform co-founded in 2018 by Aquiline Capital Partners, focused on acquiring and integrating vertical SaaS businesses serving niche small and mid-size business markets including rental management, nonprofit fundraising, self-storage, healthcare, field services, specialty retail, and association management. The company pairs proprietary embedded payment processing capabilities with specialized business management software across approximately 50,000 customer accounts through more than 70 acquired companies. Aquiline and ADIA made a major additional investment in 2023 to further accelerate the platform's expansion.
investments.end_customers: Fullsteam's end customers are small and mid-size business operators in highly specialized verticals who need purpose-built software with integrated payments. These businesses—such as rental companies, nonprofits, and independent healthcare providers—benefit from Fullsteam's deep industry-specific workflows combined with seamless payment acceptance.

investments.business_description: GS AcquisitionCo, Inc. is the legal entity name for insightsoftware, a leading provider of financial reporting, enterprise performance management (EPM), budgeting, and planning software backed by Genstar Capital, HgCapital, and TA Associates. Headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina, insightsoftware has grown to serve over 32,000 organizations globally through more than 30 product lines and 23+ acquisitions. The platform delivers an AI-enabled data virtualization layer for finance, accounting, and operations teams.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are CFOs, controllers, FP&A teams, and operations leaders at mid-market and large enterprises who need real-time financial reporting, close management, and planning tools that connect to ERPs like SAP, Oracle, and Microsoft Dynamics. Industries served include healthcare, manufacturing, financial services, and public sector.

investments.business_description: HungerRush is a cloud-based point-of-sale (POS) and restaurant management software provider designed specifically for quick-service and fast-casual restaurant brands, serving over 16,000 restaurant customers across the United States. The HungerRush 360 platform integrates order management, in-store and online ordering, delivery management, loyalty programs, inventory, marketing, and embedded payment processing into a single system. The company is backed by Corsair Capital and has roots in the restaurant POS and delivery management market.
investments.end_customers: HungerRush serves independent restaurant operators, regional chains, and multi-unit franchise operators in the quick-service and fast-casual segments who need an all-in-one technology platform to manage in-store, online, and delivery orders. Customers benefit from integrated loyalty and marketing tools that help drive repeat visits and increase ticket sizes.

investments.business_description: Newtek Merchant Solutions is the payment processing and merchant services division of NewtekOne, Inc. (NASDAQ: NEWT), a publicly traded business development company that transitioned to a bank holding company structure. The division provides small and medium-sized businesses with credit and debit card processing, ACH payments, mobile payment acceptance, EMV chip and contactless payment solutions, and fraud prevention tools, integrated with a broad suite of Newtek business services including lending, payroll, and insurance. The platform enables merchants to accept all major card networks and digital wallets including Apple Pay and Google Pay.
investments.end_customers: NewtekOne's merchant services customers are small and medium-sized businesses across a wide range of industries—retail, hospitality, professional services, and high-risk sectors—that need comprehensive and affordable payment processing with access to the broader Newtek financial services ecosystem. The company emphasizes bundled services, allowing business owners to consolidate banking, lending, and payments with a single provider.

investments.company: Project Accelerate Parent, LLC (dba ABC Fitness)
investments.investment_type: Project Accelerate Parent, LLC (dba ABC Fitness)
investments.business_description: ABC Fitness (formerly ABC Financial) is the leading global fitness club management software platform, supporting approximately 30,000 fitness facilities, 650,000 fitness coaches, and 40 million members worldwide while processing over $12 billion in payments annually. Originally founded in 1981 as a gym billing services company, ABC Fitness has been a Thoma Bravo portfolio company and has expanded through acquisitions including Evo, GymSales, Trainerize, and Glofox to cover club management, member engagement, personal training, and digital coaching. The company serves a full spectrum of fitness businesses from large multi-club chains to independent boutique studios.
investments.end_customers: ABC Fitness's customers are health club operators, gym chains, boutique fitness studios, and personal training businesses of all sizes that need integrated software for member management, billing, scheduling, and business analytics. Fitness businesses depend on ABC's platform to handle recurring membership billing, automate retention workflows, and provide digital engagement tools for their members.

investments.business_description: Eagle Family Foods Group, doing business as Eagle Foods, is a consumer food company headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio, formed in 2015 when Kelso & Company partnered with founders to acquire Eagle Brand sweetened condensed and evaporated milk from J.M. Smucker. The company has since built a multi-category branded food portfolio through acquisitions, adding Popcorn Indiana and G.H. Cretors popcorn brands in 2017 and the Helper and Suddenly Salad brands from General Mills in 2022. Eagle Foods now competes across three core categories: baking ingredients, snacks, and convenient meal kits.
investments.end_customers: Eagle Foods' brands are sold to mass-market grocery chains, club stores, and drug stores, reaching everyday consumer households across the United States. End consumers are home cooks, families, and snack buyers purchasing trusted pantry staples and branded convenience foods through mainstream retail channels.

investments.business_description: Rubix Foods (formerly Darifair Foods) is a Jacksonville, Florida-based B2B food ingredient and culinary solutions company that develops custom flavors, sauces, marinades, dairy systems, and blended ingredients for restaurant chains and food manufacturers. Founded in 1985 by the Block family as a dairy supplier, the company evolved into a full-service culinary innovation partner offering scientifically tested, chef-forward custom formulations. Arbor Investments acquired a stake in 2019 and has driven further growth through a new innovation center and expanded product capabilities.
investments.end_customers: Rubix Foods serves large restaurant chains, foodservice distributors, and food manufacturers that need proprietary, custom-developed flavor and ingredient solutions to differentiate their menu offerings. Customers are typically QSR, fast-casual, and casual-dining chains that require consistent, scalable, and customized ingredient programs rather than off-the-shelf commodity products.

investments.business_description: Tropical Cheese Industries is the leading East Coast producer and distributor of authentic Latino dairy products, founded in 1982 by Rafael Mendez and headquartered in Perth Amboy, New Jersey. The company has manufactured Hispanic-style fresh cheeses, queso fresco, chorizo, and other Latino food products for over 40 years, distributing them across the Eastern United States. A significant investment was made by private equity partners (with the Mendez family retaining a minority interest) to support continued growth.
investments.end_customers: Tropical Cheese's products are sold primarily through supermarkets, specialty grocers, and ethnic food retailers serving Hispanic and Latino consumer communities across the Eastern U.S. End consumers are Hispanic households seeking authentic, familiar dairy and charcuterie products, with the brand benefiting from long-term demographic growth in the U.S. Latino population.

investments.business_description: EPTAM Precision Solutions (formerly Eptam Plastics) is a high-precision contract manufacturer of complex medical device components and sub-assemblies, founded in 1981 and headquartered in Northfield, New Hampshire, with operations across seven states. The company specializes in precision plastic injection molding, metal machining, silicone molding, and medical-grade manufacturing for the medical device, biopharmaceutical, and industrial sectors, and has expanded its capabilities through acquisitions including Sterling Manufacturing and Albright Silicone. EPTAM serves OEM medical device companies requiring precision-manufactured single-use disposables, implants, and complex multi-material assemblies.
investments.end_customers: EPTAM's customers are medical device OEMs, biopharma instrument makers, and industrial manufacturers that outsource precision component manufacturing to meet stringent quality and regulatory standards. Healthcare equipment companies depend on EPTAM for consistently manufactured, ISO-certified components used in surgical procedures, diagnostics, drug delivery, and patient monitoring.

investments.business_description: Hamilton Thorne Ltd. (TSX: HTL) is a publicly traded Canadian company that develops, manufactures, and distributes precision instruments, laboratory equipment, consumables, software, and services for the assisted reproductive technology (ART), fertility research, and cell biology markets. The company's core products include AI-driven sperm analyzers, laser systems for embryo manipulation, IVF laboratory workstations and incubators, and media consumables, sold under brands including Hamilton Thorne, Gynemed, Planer, IVFtech, and Gynetics. Hamilton Thorne products are sold to fertility clinics, hospitals, and research institutions in approximately 100 countries.
investments.end_customers: Hamilton Thorne's customers are IVF clinics, reproductive medicine hospitals, pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology firms, and academic research institutions that require specialized laboratory instruments and consumables to perform assisted reproduction procedures. Fertility clinics depend on Hamilton Thorne's laser and imaging systems to optimize embryo and sperm selection, which are critical steps in successful IVF treatment cycles.

investments.end_customers: Hamilton Thorne's customers are IVF clinics, reproductive medicine hospitals, pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology firms, and academic research institutions that require specialized laboratory instruments and consumables to perform assisted reproduction procedures. Fertility clinics depend on Hamilton Thorne's laser and imaging systems to optimize embryo and sperm selection, which are critical steps in successful IVF treatment cycles.

investments.business_description: Riverpoint Medical is a Portland, Oregon-based developer, designer, and manufacturer of advanced biomaterial and medical textile products, including bioabsorbable and non-absorbable surgical sutures, high-strength UHMWPE fiber implants for sports medicine, brachytherapy products for oncology, and cordless LED surgical headlights (MedLED brand). Founded with a focus on proprietary biomedical textile innovation, the company was acquired by Arlington Capital Partners in 2019 and expanded its wound closure portfolio through the acquisition of CP Medical Corporation in 2024. Riverpoint serves both the human surgical market and the animal health veterinary market.
investments.end_customers: Riverpoint's customers are surgical OEMs, hospital purchasing groups, orthopedic surgeons, and veterinary practices that need specialized suture and implant components for wound closure and soft tissue repair procedures. Orthopedic and sports medicine surgeons rely on Riverpoint's high-strength fiber products for ligament and tendon repair applications.
