investments.business_description: Smarsh (backed by K1 Investment Management) is the leading electronic communications archiving and compliance platform for regulated industries, created through K1's combination of Smarsh and Actiance in 2017 and subsequent acquisitions including Digital Reasoning. The platform captures, archives, and supervises communications across email, social media, mobile messaging, instant messaging, encrypted chat, and voice — enabling firms to meet SEC, FINRA, and MiFID II surveillance and recordkeeping requirements. Smarsh serves more than 6,500 financial services firms including the top 15 global banks.
investments.end_customers: Smarsh's primary customers are compliance, legal, and records management teams at broker-dealers, investment banks, asset managers, registered investment advisors, and insurance companies that are legally required to retain and monitor employee communications. The platform is also used by government agencies and other regulated organizations. Compliance officers and supervisors use it to conduct lexicon-based surveillance, respond to regulatory inquiries, and produce records for e-discovery.

investments.business_description: Sundance Group Holdings, Inc. is the holding entity for NetDocuments, the leading cloud-based document management, email management, and collaboration software platform for law firms, corporate legal departments, and government agencies, acquired by Warburg Pincus from Clearlake Capital in 2021 for approximately $1.4 billion. NetDocuments provides a purpose-built legal cloud DMS with matter-centric organization, enterprise search, email management (including Outlook integration), team collaboration, and built-in disaster recovery, used by over 3,150 professional services firms and corporate legal departments worldwide. The platform is deeply integrated into law firm workflows and competes primarily with iManage (formerly Autonomy iManage) as one of the two dominant legal DMS platforms.
investments.end_customers: NetDocuments' customers are law firms of all sizes (from solo practitioners to Am Law 100 firms), corporate legal departments, and government legal offices that need a secure, cloud-native platform to store, organize, collaborate on, and retrieve legal documents and emails organized by client and matter. Legal secretaries, attorneys, and paralegals use NetDocuments as their daily document repository for drafting contracts, managing correspondence, and locating precedent documents, while IT teams value the cloud architecture that eliminates on-premises server management. The platform's matter-centric structure and deep Microsoft Outlook/Office integration make it deeply embedded in attorney workflows.

investments.business_description: Vamos Bidco, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle for Vermont Information Processing (VIP), a leading software provider for the beverage distribution industry founded in 1972 in Colchester, Vermont, acquired by Warburg Pincus in a deal valued at approximately $1 billion (with TowerBrook Capital Partners as the prior sponsor during the ARCC loan period). VIP's platform covers the full beverage distributor technology stack — from warehouse management and inventory control to route accounting, sales order management, EDI with suppliers and retailers, and business intelligence — serving beer, wine, and spirits distributors across the United States. The company began as a billing service for distributors in 1972 and evolved into a full-suite ERP provider for the three-tier beverage alcohol distribution system.
investments.end_customers: VIP's customers are beer, wine, and spirits wholesale distributors of all sizes — from large regional distributors operating in multiple states to smaller independent craft beer distributors — that need software purpose-built for the unique regulatory and operational requirements of the three-tier alcohol distribution system. Operations managers and salespeople at distributors use VIP's platform for route accounting, delivery management, DEX/NACS electronic invoicing, state regulatory reporting, and sales analytics that generic ERP systems cannot support. The embedded nature of route accounting software in daily delivery operations and the complexity of state-by-state alcohol compliance requirements create very high switching costs.

investments.business_description: Classic Collision is one of the largest multi-site collision repair operators in the United States, acquired by TPG Capital from New Mountain Capital in mid-2024. The company operates over 262 collision repair centers across 16 states, offering auto body repair, paint, glass repair, and ADAS (advanced driver assistance system) calibration services for all vehicle types including premium and electric vehicles. Classic Collision was founded in Atlanta in 1983 and has grown rapidly through acquisitions.
investments.end_customers: Classic Collision's end customers are vehicle owners whose cars have been damaged in collisions, predominantly directing their vehicles through insurance company direct repair program (DRP) relationships. The company works closely with major auto insurers who refer policyholders to their network shops; these DRP relationships with insurers are the primary driver of repair volume, making insurers the de facto demand channel even though vehicle owners are the direct service recipients.

investments.business_description: KUIU (pronounced 'koo-you') is a direct-to-consumer ultra-lightweight hunting gear and apparel brand founded in 2011, sold by Main Post Partners in December 2025 to a conservation-focused investor group including Cox Enterprises. KUIU designs and sells purpose-built big game hunting apparel, packs, optics accessories, and footwear — with an emphasis on lightweight performance materials, camouflage patterns, and backcountry functionality. The brand grew nearly 10x under Main Post Partners' ownership.
investments.end_customers: KUIU serves serious backcountry and big game hunters — primarily elk, deer, and bear hunters in the Western US — who prioritize ultralight weight, technical performance, and stealth in demanding mountain terrain. The brand sells exclusively direct-to-consumer through its own website and stores, targeting affluent, performance-focused hunters who are willing to pay premium prices for purpose-engineered gear.

investments.company: BCPE HIPH Parent, Inc. (dba Harrington Industrial Plastics)
investments.investment_type: BCPE HIPH Parent, Inc. (dba Harrington Industrial Plastics)
investments.business_description: Harrington Industrial Plastics (now Harrington Process Solutions), acquired by Bain Capital Private Equity in late 2023, is a leading specialty distributor of corrosion-resistant and high-purity fluid process products. With over 60 locations across the United States, Harrington distributes piping systems, pumps, valves, filtration equipment, tanks, and instrumentation — all engineered for handling corrosive or ultra-pure fluids. The company was founded in 1959 and is headquartered in Chino, California.
investments.end_customers: Harrington serves process engineers, plant engineers, and maintenance managers at facilities in semiconductor manufacturing, chemicals, food and beverage processing, water and wastewater treatment, aquatics, and life sciences. These industries require specialized non-metallic and corrosion-resistant fluid handling systems that cannot use standard steel or cast iron piping — making Harrington a critical technical distribution partner for material specification, procurement, and installation support.

investments.company: NCWS Intermediate, Inc. (dba National Carwash Solutions)
investments.investment_type: NCWS Intermediate, Inc. (dba National Carwash Solutions)
investments.business_description: National Carwash Solutions (NCS), backed by Berkshire Partners since 2021, is North America's leading full-service equipment and technology provider to the carwash industry. NCS distributes and services carwash systems, aftermarket parts, cleaning chemistry, and vacuum systems under brands including MacNeil Wash Systems, Ryko Solutions, TSS, and Shadow Equipment. Headquartered in Grimes, Iowa, with over 1,700 employees and a nationwide distribution and service footprint, NCS also acquired Zep Vehicle Care to expand into chemistry.
investments.end_customers: NCS's customers are carwash operators ranging from independent single-site operators to large multi-location express carwash chains across the US and Canada. These operators purchase carwash tunnels and equipment, replacement parts, chemicals, and subscribe to service agreements for maintenance and system uptime. The rapid build-out of express carwash chains by private equity investors has been a key demand driver for NCS's equipment platform.

investments.business_description: SouthernCarlson (owned by Truelink Capital via TL Sapphire Holdings, formerly a Kyocera subsidiary before going private) is a leading distributor and value-added services provider of fasteners, tools, packaging, and related construction and industrial supplies. The company stocks over 67,000 SKUs and operates eight strategic distribution centers and 115 local stores, serving 50,000+ professional customers across commercial construction, industrial, and residential end markets.
investments.end_customers: SouthernCarlson's primary customers are professional contractors, construction crews, framers, and industrial maintenance technicians who need reliable, rapid access to fasteners, pneumatic tools, and packaging materials on job sites. The company's local store network combined with distribution infrastructure allows contractors to procure specialty fasteners and consumable supplies faster than through big-box retailers, making same-day availability a core competitive advantage.

investments.company: UFT Buyer LLC (dba United Flow Technologies)
investments.investment_type: UFT Buyer LLC (dba United Flow Technologies)
investments.business_description: UFT Buyer LLC is the acquisition vehicle for United Flow Technologies (UFT), a technical distributor and solutions provider founded in July 2021 and headquartered in Irving, Texas that serves the municipal and industrial water and wastewater treatment markets. UFT distributes and integrates process equipment, pumps, flow control, valves, and automation solutions for water infrastructure projects, and has grown through aggressive acquisitions of regional distributors. The company was backed by H.I.G. Capital and later sold to Berkshire Partners.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are municipal water and wastewater utilities, industrial facilities, and engineering firms that purchase process equipment and integrated solutions for water treatment infrastructure. UFT's products and services help utilities maintain clean water supply and wastewater treatment compliance with EPA and state environmental regulations.

investments.business_description: Airwavz Solutions (backed by Columbia Capital) designs, installs, and operates in-building distributed antenna systems (DAS) and fiber backbone wireless infrastructure to provide 4G/5G cellular coverage and capacity inside commercial buildings. Founded in 2013 and based in Charlotte, North Carolina, Airwavz uses a neutral-host model — building a shared infrastructure that multiple wireless carriers can access — to resolve the persistent problem of poor indoor cellular coverage in large commercial properties.
investments.end_customers: Airwavz's customers are commercial real estate owners and operators — office building landlords, property trusts, hospitals, universities, stadiums, and airports — that need to guarantee strong in-building cellular service for their tenants and visitors. Wireless carriers (AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile) are the revenue-generating tenants on Airwavz's networks, paying recurring fees for access to the shared neutral-host infrastructure to offload indoor network traffic.

investments.business_description: Streamland Media is a facility-based, technology-enabled post-production services company headquartered in Los Angeles, acquired by Trive Capital in 2018. The company provides picture finishing, color grading, sound design and editing, visual effects (VFX), dailies processing, and marketing content services for feature films, episodic television, gaming, and interactive media. It operates through well-known industry brands including Picture Shop, Ghost VFX, Formosa Group, and Picture Head, with facilities across Los Angeles, Vancouver, Copenhagen, and the United Kingdom.
investments.end_customers: Streamland's clients are major film studios, streaming platforms (Netflix, Disney+, Amazon), independent production companies, and video game publishers that require high-quality post-production finishing before content release. These clients depend on Streamland's specialized facilities and technical talent for the final creative and technical stages of content production.

investments.business_description: EDB (EnterpriseDB), backed by Great Hill Partners and Bain Capital Ventures, is the leading commercial distributor and developer of enterprise-class software and managed services built on PostgreSQL — the world's most advanced open-source relational database. EDB's platform extends open-source Postgres with enterprise features including Oracle compatibility (facilitating migrations off Oracle), high availability, security, performance management, and cloud-managed database services. EDB serves over 4,000 global customers.
investments.end_customers: EDB's customers are enterprises and government agencies that rely on relational databases for mission-critical applications and are either seeking to migrate away from high-cost proprietary databases (primarily Oracle) or need enterprise-grade support and SLAs for their open-source Postgres deployments. IT leaders, DBAs, and procurement teams at financial institutions, insurers, government agencies, and technology companies purchase EDB for its Oracle compatibility layer and enterprise support guarantees.

investments.business_description: Everest Infrastructure Partners (operating through EIP Consolidated, LLC) is one of the largest independent wireless tower companies in the world, with tower portfolios in the United States, Australia, New Zealand, and Portugal. The company owns or manages more than 2,500 communications tower sites and has raised over $1.5 billion in capital commitments since its founding in 2015. In December 2023, Everest closed $600 million in senior secured credit facilities led by Goldman Sachs Private Credit to fund tower acquisitions including from Charter Communications.
investments.end_customers: Everest's customers are wireless carriers (national MNOs) and other telecommunications service providers — including broadband ISPs, utilities, and public safety entities — that lease space on Everest's towers for their antenna equipment. Tower tenants pay predictable long-term rent (typically 5-15 year leases with automatic escalators), making tower companies a highly contracted, inflation-linked infrastructure asset class.

investments.business_description: K2 Towers is an independent wireless tower company founded in 2009 and backed by Shamrock Capital Advisors and Peppertree Capital (TPG Peppertree), focused on the acquisition and operation of wireless communications towers across the United States. The company consolidated towers from a variety of sources — including a notable $680 million acquisition of AT&T towers — and grew its portfolio to thousands of sites before being acquired by American Tower in December 2023.
investments.end_customers: K2 Towers's customers are the major US wireless carriers — AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile, and regional operators — that lease space on towers to mount their network antennas. Carriers use independent tower companies like K2 to site, permit, build, and maintain their radio access network infrastructure, paying long-term contractual rent in exchange for reliable access to strategically located tower assets.

investments.business_description: Octagon Towers LLC is an independent wireless tower company formed in 2019 as a subsidiary of Peppertree Capital (subsequently acquired by TPG in 2025, becoming part of TPG Peppertree). Octagon was created to hold and operate the approximately 1,000 towers purchased from AT&T for $680 million in 2019 — towers that AT&T had built primarily after its last large tower sale to Crown Castle in 2013. The portfolio consists of relatively modern towers located across the US.
investments.end_customers: Octagon's primary tenants are the major US wireless carriers, including AT&T (the original seller, which leased-back its tower positions) and other national and regional operators seeking space on strategically located tower assets. Tower companies collect long-term, inflation-escalating leases from carrier tenants, generating highly predictable infrastructure cash flows.

investments.business_description: Skyway Towers is an independent wireless tower company based in Tampa, Florida that specializes in build-to-suit tower construction, tower acquisition, and colocation for wireless carriers, broadband ISPs, government agencies, and other spectrum users. The company has over 480 tower sites built or under development spanning 30 states, offering both new construction and existing tower colocation options. Skyway was acquired by Basalt Infrastructure Partners and in January 2024 closed $200 million in senior secured funding to continue its expansion.
investments.end_customers: Skyway's customers are wireless carriers (national and regional), fixed wireless broadband providers, microwave backhaul operators, and government/public safety radio users that need new tower construction or access to existing tower infrastructure. Build-to-suit customers engage Skyway to design and construct a tower meeting specific coverage and structural requirements, then lease space on a long-term basis once the tower is operational.

investments.business_description: Tarpon Towers (originally Florida Tower Partners, founded 2008) is an independent wireless tower acquisition and development company backed by private equity investors including Spire Capital Partners and Redwood Capital Investments, focused on building and acquiring communications tower assets across the US. The company raised over $50 million from ABS Capital and built a multi-thousand-site portfolio through acquisitions and new development before being acquired by American Tower in February 2024.
investments.end_customers: Tarpon Towers's customers are wireless carriers and broadband service providers seeking space on towers for their antenna equipment. Carriers enter multi-year, renewal-protected lease agreements for tower space, providing Tarpon with contracted recurring revenue. American Tower's acquisition of Tarpon reflects the continued consolidation of the independent tower sector by large publicly traded tower REITs seeking to grow their US tower portfolios.

investments.business_description: Towerco IV Holdings LLC is a wireless tower infrastructure holding company, part of the broader TowerCo platform — one of the largest privately held wireless infrastructure companies in the United States with over 25 years of industry expertise and approximately 15,000 tower, land, and rooftop assets nationwide. TowerCo jointly acquired 546 towers from Charter Communications with Everest Infrastructure Partners starting in December 2023, financed in part by Goldman Sachs Private Credit. The 'IV' designation indicates it is one of multiple fund vehicles within the TowerCo investment structure.
investments.end_customers: TowerCo's tenants are US wireless carriers and other wireless service providers that pay long-term lease fees for space on TowerCo's tower and rooftop assets to mount their antenna and radio equipment. Infrastructure investors are attracted to the tower model's highly contracted, inflation-linked revenue streams with significant operating leverage from colocation — the ability to add tenants to an existing tower with minimal incremental cost.

investments.business_description: MPI Engineered Technologies (backed by Industrial Growth Partners) is a manufacturer of high-precision fine-blanked metal components for automotive, agricultural, commercial truck, and industrial applications. Fine blanking is a specialized metal stamping process that produces components with tight tolerances and superior edge quality, making it suitable for safety-critical and precision-critical applications such as powertrain, transmission, chassis, axle, and hydraulic pump components. MPI also serves medical device and industrial markets.
investments.end_customers: MPI's customers are Tier 1 and Tier 2 automotive suppliers and OEMs that require precision metal stampings for powertrain, transmission, chassis, seating, and exhaust systems. Agricultural equipment manufacturers, commercial truck makers, and hydraulic equipment OEMs are additional end markets. These customers specify fine blanking for components that cannot tolerate the edge imperfections of conventional stamping — essentially outsourcing precision component manufacturing to MPI.

investments.business_description: Wine.com is the world's largest online wine retailer, founded in 1998 and headquartered in San Francisco, offering over 10,000 wines shipped to consumers across nearly all 50 states. The company operates a direct-to-consumer e-commerce model with a subscription loyalty program (StewardShip offering free shipping) and editorial content including expert ratings and food pairing recommendations. Wine.com generated an estimated $283 million in US e-commerce net sales in 2023, ranking second in its category.
investments.end_customers: Wine.com's customers are wine enthusiasts and gift buyers who prefer the breadth, convenience, and editorial guidance of a specialized online wine retailer over a local liquor store or supermarket wine aisle. The platform is especially popular for premium and hard-to-find wines, gift purchases (corporate and consumer), and subscription-oriented wine shoppers who value curated discovery over price competition.

investments.business_description: Senneca Holdings (acquired by Kohlberg & Company from Audax Private Equity in 2018, originally Chase Industries) is a manufacturer of specialty industrial doors, headquartered in Cincinnati, Ohio, operating twelve manufacturing facilities. The company's portfolio of brands — Chase, Eliason, TMI, Thermoseal, SubZero, Curtron, and others — covers traffic doors, flexible strip doors, specialty glass doors, cold storage doors, and data center enclosures. All products are built-to-order for specific industrial and commercial applications.
investments.end_customers: Senneca's customers are facility managers, construction contractors, and procurement teams at food processing plants, cold storage warehouses, distribution centers, retail and food service facilities, pharmaceutical manufacturing plants, and data centers — all of which require specialty doors to manage temperature, hygiene, traffic flow, and energy efficiency. The product line's critical function in food safety compliance and cold chain integrity makes it a recurring replacement and new construction purchase.

investments.business_description: Sweep Midco LLC is the holding entity for Sweeping Corporation of America (SCA), backed by Warburg Pincus since November 2020. SCA is the largest outsourced provider of street and parking lot sweeping services in the United States, offering mechanical broom and regenerative air vacuum sweeping services that remove debris from roads, parking lots, and runways — providing cost-effective environmental solutions that reduce stormwater pollution and improve air quality. SCA serves municipalities, DOTs, airports, and private property owners.
investments.end_customers: SCA's customers are municipalities and state DOTs (that contract for street sweeping as part of stormwater NPDES permit compliance), airports (that sweep runways for FOD — foreign object debris — safety requirements), commercial property managers (parking lots and private roads), and industrial facility operators. Municipal contracts provide stable recurring revenue; environmental regulatory requirements mandate regular sweeping in many jurisdictions, creating non-discretionary demand.

investments.business_description: Wine.com, Inc. is the same operating business as Wine.com, LLC — the world's largest online wine retailer, offering over 10,000 wine SKUs shipped directly to consumers across nearly all 50 states. The existence of both LLC and Inc. entities in GSBD's portfolio (one as second lien debt, one as unsecured debt) reflects different tranches of the company's capital structure backed by the same underlying operating business. Wine.com generated an estimated $283 million in US online wine sales in 2023.
investments.end_customers: Wine.com's end customers are wine enthusiasts, corporate gift buyers, and subscription wine shoppers who purchase directly through the company's e-commerce platform. The StewardShip membership program drives loyalty among frequent purchasers, while the company's breadth of selection (10,000+ labels) and expert editorial guidance attract consumers seeking wines beyond what is available at local retailers.

investments.business_description: Bayside Parent operates as a holding company for healthcare service providers, potentially encompassing skilled nursing facilities, rehabilitation centers, and long-term care services across multiple states. The parent entity consolidates related healthcare operations.
investments.end_customers: Patients requiring post-acute care, rehabilitation services, and skilled nursing care, as well as their families seeking quality long-term healthcare solutions.

investments.business_description: mPulse is a health technology company providing AI-powered analytics, omnichannel engagement, and digital health navigation solutions for healthcare organizations to activate populations and improve health outcomes.
investments.end_customers: mPulse serves healthcare organizations, health plans, and insurance companies seeking digital engagement solutions and personalized health navigation for population health management.

investments.business_description: Prairie Provident Resources is a Calgary-based publicly traded oil and gas exploration and production company listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX: PPR). The company focuses on conventional light and medium oil production in Alberta's Western Canadian Sedimentary Basin, primarily in the Michichi, Princess, and Provost areas. Its Michichi core area encompasses approximately 168,000 net acres with dozens of identified Basal Quartz drilling opportunities.
investments.end_customers: Prairie Provident sells crude oil and natural gas to midstream purchasers and refiners in the Canadian market. Its end buyers are wholesale crude oil purchasers and pipeline operators; ultimate consumers include Canadian refineries that process Alberta light and medium crude into transportation fuels and petroleum products.

investments.business_description: VisionSafe Parent is the holding company for VisionSafe LLC, an aerospace safety company that designs and manufactures the Emergency Vision Assurance System (EVAS). EVAS is a self-contained cockpit safety device that inflates a transparent vision unit to displace smoke from a pilot's line of sight during a smoke-in-the-cockpit emergency, allowing the crew to fly the aircraft to a safe landing. The system is certified for over 120 aircraft types across 10 OEMs, with nearly 7,000 units protecting approximately 3,500 aircraft worldwide. The company is backed by Crescendo Capital Partners.
investments.end_customers: Commercial and corporate aviation operators — including cargo carriers, business jet operators, and corporate flight departments — who purchase EVAS as a safety supplement or factory option. Bombardier, Dassault Falcon Jet, and Gulfstream Aerospace offer EVAS as a standard client option; over 72% of the world's cargo aircraft fleet flies with the system installed.

investments.business_description: Elah Holdings (OTC: ELLH) is a Dallas-based shell holding company that seeks to acquire profitable businesses in commercial and industrial markets, primarily to deploy its significant net operating loss (NOL) tax assets. Formerly known as Real Industry, Inc., the company previously operated aluminum melting, processing, and recycling businesses before pivoting to a pure acquisition-oriented model after rebranding in 2018. It has acquired Beck Aluminum Alloys assets and continues to evaluate targets across commercial, industrial, and financial sectors.
investments.end_customers: As a holding company, Elah does not have direct end customers of its own. Its portfolio companies serve industrial and commercial buyers; the company's primary value proposition to investors lies in its ability to shelter acquired earnings through substantial accumulated NOL carryforwards.

investments.company: RPC ABC Investment Holdings LLC (dba ABC Plumbing)
investments.investment_type: RPC ABC Investment Holdings LLC (dba ABC Plumbing)
investments.business_description: RPC ABC Investment Holdings is the private equity holding structure for ABC Plumbing, Sewer, Heating, Cooling & Electric, a multi-decade-old home services provider headquartered in the Chicago suburbs (Arlington Heights, Illinois). ABC has operated for over 70 years offering residential and commercial plumbing, sewer, HVAC, and electrical services across Illinois and Florida. Roark Capital Group, a private equity firm specializing in franchise and multi-location consumer services businesses, is the financial sponsor.
investments.end_customers: Residential homeowners and commercial property owners in the greater Chicago area and Florida who need plumbing, drain, HVAC installation and repair, and electrical services. The customer base is largely repeat or recurring, driven by emergency service calls, seasonal HVAC needs, and ongoing maintenance agreements.

investments.company: SEM Holdings, LLC (dba Southeast Mechanical, LLC)
investments.investment_type: SEM Holdings, LLC (dba Southeast Mechanical, LLC)
investments.business_description: SEM Holdings is the holding company for Southeast Mechanical, an operator-driven HVAC services platform headquartered in North Carolina and backed by Palladin Consumer Retail Partners since 2022. The company was built through the combination of four core HVAC contractors in North Carolina — Triangle Contractors, Warren-Hay Mechanical, Airmakers Heating & Cooling, and Logan Heating & Air — and has subsequently expanded via acquisitions including Climate Design Home Services in Clearwater, Florida. The platform employs over 300 HVAC professionals and serves more than 11,000 residential and commercial customers.
investments.end_customers: Residential homeowners and light commercial property owners primarily in the Carolinas and Florida requiring HVAC installation, maintenance, and emergency repair services. Customers value regional brand trust and responsive service; commercial clients include property managers and small businesses with ongoing service contracts.

investments.business_description: Whitewater Holding Company is the parent entity for WhiteWater Express Car Wash, a Houston-based express exterior car wash chain founded in 2016. The business owns and operates a growing network of express car wash locations, generating a significant portion of revenue through recurring monthly unlimited wash memberships. Freeman Spogli & Co. recapitalized the company in late 2021, with Goldman Sachs and Blue Owl providing debt financing; Goldman Sachs's BDC arm (GSBD) holds an equity position.
investments.end_customers: Individual consumers seeking convenient, affordable, and fast exterior car washing, primarily through monthly subscription memberships. Locations are positioned along high-traffic corridors in suburban markets; the subscription model targets commuters and everyday drivers who wash their vehicles frequently.

investments.business_description: Iracore International Holdings is a Wind Point Partners-backed manufacturer specializing in rubber and polyurethane pipe lining systems used to protect steel pipe from abrasion and corrosion in demanding industrial environments. Founded in 1957 and headquartered in Hibbing, Minnesota, the company produces lined pipe spools, fittings, and custom wear components ranging from 3 to 72 inches in diameter and up to 63 feet in length. Its patented dual-layer natural rubber and polyurethane lining systems are engineered specifically for oil sands slurry transport, tailings disposal, mining, and other high-abrasion applications.
investments.end_customers: Oil sands producers, hard rock mining operations, and industrial plant operators that require durable piping solutions for abrasive and corrosive slurry transport. Major end markets include Canadian oil sands operators (Alberta) and mineral processing facilities where standard steel pipe would fail rapidly due to abrasion or chemical attack.

investments.business_description: Streamland Media Holdings is the parent company of Streamland Media, a Los Angeles-based global post-production services group owned by Trive Capital and Five Crowns Capital. Operating under brands including Picture Shop, The Farm, Finalé, Ghost VFX, Formosa Group, Ingenuity Studios, and Sim Post Production, the company provides critical post-production services — visual effects (VFX), color grading, editorial finishing, sound design, and interactive content — to feature film and episodic television studios worldwide. The business was assembled through a series of acquisitions including Technicolor Post and Sim Video International's post-production operations.
investments.end_customers: Major Hollywood studios, streaming platforms, and independent production companies that require post-production services for feature films, episodic television series, and interactive (video game) content. Clients include the major streaming services and legacy studio distributors who depend on Streamland's facilities and artist talent for the final stages of content production.

investments.business_description: PPT Management Holdings is a management holding entity for Professional Physical Therapy (Pro-PT), the same outpatient rehabilitation network associated with the Bayside Parent holding structure, both backed by Revelstoke Capital Partners. Professional Physical Therapy operates over 200 outpatient clinics in New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, Massachusetts, and New Hampshire, providing physical therapy, hand therapy, and occupational therapy for orthopedic, post-surgical, and sports-related conditions. The PPT Management entity likely holds operational management functions or certain equity layers within the multi-entity holding structure.
investments.end_customers: Patients across the Northeast recovering from orthopedic injuries, surgical procedures, sports injuries, and musculoskeletal conditions. Referrals are driven by orthopedic surgeons and primary care physicians; payers include commercial health insurers, workers' compensation carriers, and Medicare.

investments.industry: Independent Power and Renewable Electricity Producers
investments.business_description: Power Factors is the global leader in renewable energy asset performance management software, providing owners and operators of wind, solar, and energy storage assets with enterprise software for performance monitoring, commercial asset management, and workflow analytics. The platform manages over 110 gigawatts of renewable assets worldwide across its Drive, Greenbyte, and BluePoint product lines. Vista Equity Partners acquired the company in 2021, accelerating its expansion through the prior mergers of Power Factors with Greenbyte AB and 3megawatt GmbH.
investments.end_customers: Utility-scale renewable energy owners, independent power producers, and asset managers use Power Factors to monitor fleet performance, optimize energy output, and manage O&M workflows across their wind and solar portfolios. Project finance lenders and infrastructure funds with exposure to renewable assets also rely on the platform for portfolio-level performance visibility.

investments.business_description: Pluralsight is an online technology workforce development platform offering skill assessment, expert-authored video courses, and engineering analytics tools covering cloud, AI/ML, security, data science, and software development. The company serves enterprise customers seeking to upskill technology teams and was taken private by Vista Equity Partners for $3.5 billion in 2021. Following covenant pressure and a 2024 restructuring, a consortium of lenders led by Blue Owl Capital, Ares Management, BlackRock, and Goldman Sachs took full ownership, erasing Vista's equity.
investments.end_customers: Enterprise technology organizations, software development teams, and IT departments at large companies use Pluralsight to assess skill gaps, deliver targeted learning paths, and track technology workforce development. CTOs, CIOs, and learning and development leaders are the primary buyers, often deploying Pluralsight as an enterprise license across engineering and IT teams.

investments.company: Abacus Data Holdings, Inc. (dba Clutch Intermediate Holdings)
investments.investment_type: Abacus Data Holdings, Inc. (dba Clutch Intermediate Holdings)
investments.business_description: Clutch is a B2C customer data and retention marketing platform serving large consumer-facing brands, providing data management, loyalty program management, marketing automation, stored value, and CRM capabilities integrated across in-store, online, mobile, and social channels. The platform impacts over 500 brands and 65 million consumers globally and is integrated with Salesforce and other enterprise CRM systems. Comvest Partners, a middle-market PE firm, backed Clutch as a data-driven loyalty and customer marketing platform.
investments.end_customers: Retailers, restaurant chains, hospitality companies, and other B2C brands use Clutch to build and operate loyalty programs, deliver personalized marketing offers, and analyze customer purchasing behavior. Brand marketing and customer retention teams at mid-to-large consumer businesses are the primary buyers.

investments.business_description: Wine.com is the world's largest online wine retailer, founded in 1998 and headquartered in San Francisco, offering over 10,000 wines shipped to consumers across nearly all 50 states. The company operates a direct-to-consumer e-commerce model with a subscription loyalty program (StewardShip offering free shipping) and editorial content including expert ratings and food pairing recommendations. Wine.com generated an estimated $283 million in US e-commerce net sales in 2023, ranking second in its category.
investments.end_customers: Wine.com's customers are wine enthusiasts and gift buyers who prefer the breadth, convenience, and editorial guidance of a specialized online wine retailer over a local liquor store or supermarket wine aisle. The platform is especially popular for premium and hard-to-find wines, gift purchases (corporate and consumer), and subscription-oriented wine shoppers who value curated discovery over price competition.

investments.company: FS WhiteWater Holdings, LLC (fka Whitewater Holding Company LLC)
investments.investment_type: FS WhiteWater Holdings, LLC (fka Whitewater Holding Company LLC)
investments.business_description: FS WhiteWater Holdings (formerly Whitewater Holding Company LLC) is a renamed holding entity for WhiteWater Express Car Wash, a Houston-based express exterior car wash chain operating a growing network of locations across the Sun Belt and adjacent markets. The business is co-sponsored by Freeman Spogli & Co. (equity) with debt financing from Goldman Sachs and Blue Owl Capital; GSBD holds preferred equity in this holding layer. Revenue is driven predominantly by recurring monthly unlimited wash memberships.
investments.end_customers: Individual consumers seeking fast, affordable express exterior car washing, typically through subscription-based unlimited monthly plans. Target customers are suburban commuters and everyday vehicle owners in high-traffic markets who value convenience and price predictability over full-service detailing.

investments.company: SDB HOLDCO, LLC (dba Specialty Dental Brands)
investments.investment_type: SDB HOLDCO, LLC (dba Specialty Dental Brands)
investments.business_description: SDB HoldCo is the parent holding company for Specialty Dental Brands, one of the largest multi-specialty dental support organizations (DSOs) in the United States, operating in 25 states. Originally backed by Varsity Healthcare Partners, TSG Consumer Partners, and Leon Capital Group, Specialty Dental Brands provides non-clinical business support services — billing, staffing, marketing, compliance — to dental practices specializing in oral surgery, pediatric dentistry, and orthodontics. Following a debt default and financial restructuring approved in 2024, ownership was transferred primarily to its lender group, with management and dentist-owners retaining a 45% stake.
investments.end_customers: Dental patients seeking specialty oral health services — specifically oral surgery (wisdom teeth, implants), orthodontic treatment (braces, aligners), and pediatric dental care — across 25 states. The affiliated dental practices primarily serve commercially insured and self-pay patients, with some Medicaid-covered pediatric dental patients depending on the market.

investments.business_description: Khoros is a customer engagement software company providing social media management, community management, contact center, and customer care solutions for enterprise brands. The company serves global enterprises with tools for social marketing, customer service, and online community building. (Acquired by IgniteTech in May 2025)
investments.end_customers: Enterprise brands and agencies requiring integrated social media management, customer care automation, community management, and omnichannel customer engagement solutions.

investments.business_description: CloudBees is an enterprise software delivery platform built around Jenkins, the world's most widely used open-source CI/CD automation framework (estimated 11 million+ developer users). CloudBees layers enterprise capabilities on top of Jenkins — including centralized governance, high availability, security controls, automated plugin management, and compliance features — enabling large organizations to scale Jenkins across thousands of development teams. The company was VC-backed by Goldman Sachs, Delta-v Capital, and Golub Capital.
investments.end_customers: CloudBees targets large enterprises and highly regulated organizations (financial services, defense, healthcare, government) that have standardized on Jenkins for software delivery but need enterprise-grade reliability, auditability, and support. Customers are typically heads of DevOps, platform engineering, or software delivery who need to centrally manage hundreds of Jenkins instances while maintaining security and compliance.

investments.business_description: Prophix Software (the operating company behind the Pound Bidco holding entity) is a Canadian corporate performance management (CPM) and financial planning software company founded in 1987 and headquartered in Mississauga, Ontario, offering a cloud-based platform for budgeting, planning, forecasting, consolidation, and financial reporting. The Prophix One platform serves over 3,000 active customers in more than 40 countries, primarily in the mid-market segment. Hg Capital acquired a majority stake in Prophix in a deal valued at approximately $500–600 million in January 2021.
investments.end_customers: Customers are finance and accounting teams at mid-market companies across industries — manufacturing, professional services, retail, healthcare, and financial services — who need purpose-built software to replace spreadsheet-based budgeting and financial reporting processes. CFOs and FP&A directors choose Prophix for its ease of deployment, robust workflow automation, and strong ROI relative to enterprise-tier CPM solutions.

investments.business_description: Everest Clinical Research Corporation is a full-service contract research organization (CRO) founded in 2004 and headquartered in Toronto, Ontario, with offices in New Jersey and Shanghai, providing end-to-end clinical trial support including regulatory strategy, clinical operations, data management, biostatistics, pharmacovigilance, and medical writing to pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and medical device companies. The company has grown to over 700 employees serving more than 130 active clients globally. ArchiMed Group, a European healthcare-focused private equity firm, is listed as the sponsor in the GSBD portfolio.
investments.end_customers: Customers are pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and medical device companies — ranging from emerging biotechs to established pharma — who outsource clinical trial design, execution, and regulatory submission support to Everest. Sponsors choose Everest for its high-quality deliverables, flexibility in engagement models, and particular strength in North American and Asia-Pacific regulatory pathways.

investments.business_description: Absorb Software (the operating company behind Rodeo Buyer Company) is a cloud-based Learning Management System (LMS) provider founded in Calgary, Alberta, Canada, serving over 23 million users across more than 1,400 global clients in 34 countries with offices in North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific. The Absorb LMS platform enables organizations to create, deliver, track, and report on employee training, compliance programs, and extended enterprise learning for customers and partners. Welsh, Carson, Anderson & Stowe (WCAS) acquired Absorb from Silversmith Capital Partners in 2021 for approximately US$500 million.
investments.end_customers: Customers are HR, L&D (learning and development), and compliance teams at mid-to-large enterprises across industries including healthcare, financial services, manufacturing, and technology who need a scalable, modern LMS to manage internal training programs, regulatory compliance training, and external training for customers or channel partners. Absorb is selected for its ease of administration, learner experience, and robust reporting.

investments.business_description: Absorb Software (the operating company behind Rodeo Buyer Company) is a cloud-based Learning Management System (LMS) provider founded in Calgary, Alberta, Canada, serving over 23 million users across more than 1,400 global clients in 34 countries with offices in North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific. Absorb is selected for its ease of administration, learner experience, and robust reporting.

investments.business_description: iWave Information Systems (now rebranded as Kindsight following a merger with UC Innovation) is a fundraising intelligence software company that aggregates wealth, philanthropic, and biographic data to help nonprofit organizations identify, qualify, and prioritize major gift prospects. The platform replaces manual donor research with automated prospect screening, predictive scoring, and customizable donor profiles, and integrates with major CRM systems including Salesforce, Raiser's Edge, and others. iWave has been consistently rated the top fundraising intelligence solution and recently added generative AI capabilities through the acquisition of NonprofitOS.
investments.end_customers: Customers are major gift officers, development teams, and advancement offices at universities, hospitals, nonprofits, and faith-based organizations who need to identify which donors have the wealth, philanthropic interest, and affinity to make significant gifts. Organizations use iWave to prioritize cultivation efforts, set ask amounts, and surface new prospects from their existing donor databases.

investments.business_description: Kawa Solar Holdings acquired the Conergy brand and international solar operations in 2013, developing and operating global photovoltaic power plants and solar installations. The company specializes in the development, construction, and long-term asset management of commercial and utility-scale solar systems.
investments.end_customers: Kawa Solar's end customers are corporate and industrial energy consumers, utility-scale project developers, and asset owners seeking long-term solar power generation. Customers span commercial real estate, manufacturing, power generation facilities, and energy infrastructure investors in multiple countries including US, Canada, UK, Australia, and Asia.

investments.business_description: ClearCourse Partnership is a UK-based buy-and-build software and payments group backed by Aquiline Capital Partners, targeting small and mid-sized businesses across five divisions: Business Services, Events & Leisure, Membership Services, Payments, and Retail & Hospitality. The company has assembled over 30 software products through more than a dozen acquisitions since its founding in 2018, serving more than 20,000 customers across the UK and Ireland. Its solutions handle membership management, event booking, payments processing, and ERP-style business operations for SMEs.
investments.end_customers: ClearCourse's end customers are SMEs in sectors such as sports clubs, trade associations, leisure venues, retailers, and hospitality operators that rely on its vertical software to run daily operations including scheduling, payments, and member management. The breadth of verticals means no single industry dominates the customer base.
